title: Substack vs Campaign Monitor (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack and Campaign Monitor. We break down pricing, features, and who wins for newsletters, agencies, and ecommerce.
h1: Substack vs Campaign Monitor: Head-to-Head Comparison
intro: Substack and Campaign Monitor serve fundamentally different email marketing needs. Substack is designed for independent writers and creators looking to monetize newsletters directly, while Campaign Monitor caters to agencies and businesses managing professional email campaigns. Understanding their core differences will help you choose the right platform for your goals.

comparison_table.tool_a: Revenue-share (free to start, 10% cut on paid subscriptions)

comparison_table.tool_b: Agencies and teams managing multiple brands

comparison_table.tool_a: Paid subscriptions, podcast hosting, notes feed, built-in audience discovery
comparison_table.tool_b: Journey designer, smart segments, multi-brand management, transactional email

choose_a_if: You're an independent writer or creator wanting to monetize content through paid subscriptions with zero upfront costs
You want a built-in audience discovery feature and don't need advanced segmentation or multi-brand management
You're comfortable with a revenue-share model and prefer simplicity over extensive email marketing tools
choose_b_if: You're an agency or business managing multiple client brands from one dashboard
You need advanced features like journey designer, smart segments, transactional email, and detailed analytics
You prefer a transparent, predictable monthly pricing model over revenue-sharing arrangements

faq.q: Is Substack better than Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Neither is universally 'better'—they serve different purposes. Substack excels for individual creators monetizing newsletters with a built-in audience, while Campaign Monitor is superior for agencies and businesses needing professional email marketing infrastructure. Choose based on your use case, not reputation.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Substack is cheaper for most users since it's free to start and only takes a 10% commission on paid subscriptions you actually make. Campaign Monitor requires a minimum $11/month subscription regardless of email volume, making it costlier for beginners but more predictable for established businesses.

faq.q: Can I switch from Campaign Monitor to Substack?
faq.a: You can export your subscriber lists from Campaign Monitor and import them into Substack, but the platforms serve different purposes. Substack focuses on paid newsletter monetization, while Campaign Monitor targets marketing campaigns, so migration depends on whether Substack's model aligns with your goals.
